title: [Bug]: Backlog sync accepts non-standard JSON constants
summary: The backlog sync tool can parse local backlog JSON containing Python-specific constants such as NaN or Infinity, especially when they appear in ignored extra fields.
observed: A backlog item with an extra NaN field can pass validation because the field is ignored after parsing.
expected: Backlog sync should treat non-standard JSON constants as invalid input regardless of whether the field is later used.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that adds an ignored NaN field to a backlog item JSON document and calls load_backlog_item.
impact: The automation should not normalize or ignore malformed JSON in public backlog source material. Strict parsing keeps issue generation deterministic and auditable.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix hardens GitHub issue automation only.
security: The fix must keep existing redaction and public-safety validation in place and must not disclose private local details.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves non-standard constants in backlog JSON are rejected.
Valid backlog files continue to load.
The error message clearly identifies invalid JSON.
Existing backlog sync tests and full checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, backlog sync tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and backlog workflow documentation if public behavior changes.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
